nl_statement: If f has gradient f'(x) at every x ∈ s, then for every m : ℝ the function g(x) = f(x) - (m/2)·‖x‖² has gradient (f'(x) - m·x) at every x ∈ s.
lean_signature: theorem sub_normsquare_gradient_helper_depth1_idx1 {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {s : Set E} {f : E → ℝ} {f' : E → E} (hf : ∀ x ∈ s, HasGradientAt f (f' x) x) (m : ℝ) : ∀ x ∈ s, HasGradientAt (fun x => f x - m / 2 * ‖x‖ ^ 2) (f' x - m • x) x
assumptions: f has gradient f' on s
reuse_reason: needed for any reduction StrongConvex → Convex on f - (m/2)‖·‖²
